codeQuality.notes: Well-structured implementation with all sub-components co-located in a single file as specified. Follows amber/gold theme, glass-morphic panel conventions, and Framer Motion AnimatePresence patterns consistently. Good error handling with try/catch + toast. Minor gaps: image-mode upload is wired in the UI but not passed through to the API, bottom prompt bar is decorative-only, and buildRequestForStudio in helpers.ts has no worldview case (non-blocking since worldview uses its own generation flow).

codeReview.summary: The implementation covers all required files and expected behaviors. KanvasStudio type union extended, helpers updated (order, meta, normalizeStudioParam), KanvasPage conditionally renders WorldviewSection. The WorldviewSection component includes all specified sub-components: SceneStrip, SceneChip, WorldCreatorPanel, WorldGeneratingCard, WorldCard, TakeCard, GeneratedImageCard, and WorldviewCanvas. Error handling is proper with try/catch, toast.error, and inline error state with retry. 12 new tests pass covering integration and store operations.

issues.description: buildRequestForStudio() switch statement has no case for 'worldview'. Since KanvasStudio now includes 'worldview', calling this function with studio='worldview' would return undefined. Not currently called for worldview (it uses its own generation flow via worldLabsService), but this is a maintenance hazard. A default throw or exhaustive check would prevent future bugs.

KANVAS_STUDIO_ORDER.map(entry => fetchKanvasModels(entry)) now includes 'worldview', triggering an unnecessary API call to fetch Kanvas models for the worldview studio (which uses its own Marble model system, not Kanvas models). The result at index 4 is silently discarded. Harmless but wasteful.

handleCreateWorld receives _imageFile parameter (prefixed with underscore) but never passes it to worldLabsService.generateWorld(). The image upload UI in WorldCreatorPanel works (file select + preview) but the file is not actually sent to the API. This means the 'Image' prompt mode in the creator panel appears functional but doesn't actually use the uploaded image for world generation.

handleRetryGeneration only clears the error state (setGenerationError(null)), which causes the UI to return to the CTA/creator panel. It does not actually re-invoke the generation. The naming suggests retry behavior. This is a reasonable UX choice but slightly misleading.
buildRequestForStudio() lacks a 'worldview' case in switch — returns undefined if called with worldview studio. Not currently triggered but a maintenance hazard.
fetchKanvasModels called for 'worldview' studio unnecessarily during page load. Result is discarded.
Image file from WorldCreatorPanel is accepted in UI but never forwarded to the API service. Image prompt mode is partially functional.
Bottom prompt bar input at line ~470 has no onChange handler or state — it's purely decorative scaffolding. Consider adding a TODO comment or disabling it.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md lists files allowed to be modified but does not document the implicit constraint that buildRequestForStudio() needs updating when new studios are added to KanvasStudio. This is a cross-cutting concern that could catch future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/features/kanvas/helpers.ts:356 — buildRequestForStudio has a switch over KanvasStudio but no exhaustive check. Adding 'worldview' to the type union without updating this function was safe only because worldview bypasses this codepath.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md doesn't mention that KANVAS_STUDIO_ORDER is iterated to call fetchKanvasModels() in KanvasPage useEffect. Adding a new studio to the order triggers an API call for that studio's models. Future studios that don't use Kanvas models (like worldview) should be filtered out or handled.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/pages/KanvasPage.tsx:750 — ...KANVAS_STUDIO_ORDER.map(entry => fetchKanvasModels(entry)) now fetches for worldview, with the result at index 4 silently ignored.

summary: The worldview-studio-ui feature is well-implemented and passes review. All required files were created and modified per specification. The WorldviewSection component contains all expected sub-components (SceneStrip, SceneChip, WorldCreatorPanel, WorldGeneratingCard, WorldCard, TakeCard, GeneratedImageCard, WorldviewCanvas) with proper amber/gold theming, glass-morphic panels, and Framer Motion animations. KanvasPage integration is clean with conditional rendering for the worldview studio. Error handling includes try/catch, toast notifications, and inline error states with retry. 12 new tests verify studio integration and scene management. Non-blocking issues: buildRequestForStudio missing worldview case, unnecessary fetchKanvasModels call for worldview, and image-mode upload not wired to the API. No blocking issues found.
